title: Supreme Court: Walking on Demarcated Footpaths is a Fundamental Right
summary: The Supreme Court of India ruled that citizens have a fundamental right to walk on demarcated footpaths, linking pedestrian access to the right to life under Article 21 of the Constitution. The court observed that encroachments on footpaths by hawkers, vehicles, and illegal structures violate this right. The judgment reinforces the state's obligation to keep public pedestrian infrastructure free from obstruction. This ruling expands the jurisprudential scope of Article 21 to include the right to safe mobility.
exam_angle: This ruling is significant for understanding the expansive interpretation of Article 21 (Right to Life) by the Supreme Court, a recurring theme in GS Paper 2 questions on Fundamental Rights and judicial activism.
key_facts: Court: Supreme Court of India
Constitutional provision: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Right recognised: Walking on demarcated footpaths as a Fundamental Right
Context: Encroachments on public footpaths by hawkers and vehicles

title: Supreme Court Upholds Government's Temporary Blocking of Telegram, Directs Fund for Young Lawyers
summary: The Supreme Court held that the Indian government's temporary blocking of the Telegram app was not disproportionate, dismissing Telegram's legal challenge. The court noted that the blocking was done under the Information Technology Act, 2000, and was proportionate to the stated security and law-enforcement concerns. In a separate but related development on judicial reform, the Supreme Court flagged 'brain drain' from the legal profession and directed the creation of a welfare fund for young lawyers. The fund is intended to support junior advocates who face financial hardship in the early years of their career.
exam_angle: The Telegram ruling tests knowledge of IT Act provisions (Section 69A) on blocking digital content and the proportionality doctrine in fundamental rights jurisprudence. The young lawyers' fund touches on Bar Council reforms and access to justice.
key_facts: Law invoked for blocking: Information Technology Act, 2000 (Section 69A)
Court upheld: Government's temporary ban on Telegram as proportionate
SC also directed: Creation of a welfare fund for young/junior lawyers
Issue flagged: 'Brain drain' from the legal profession

title: Jio IPO Approved by Board; DRHP to be Filed with SEBI on June 19, 2026
summary: Reliance Industries Limited (RIL) Chairman Mukesh Ambani announced at the RIL Annual General Meeting (AGM) 2026 that the board has approved the Initial Public Offering (IPO) of Jio Platforms. The Draft Red Herring Prospectus (DRHP) is to be filed with the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) on June 19, 2026. If it proceeds, the Jio IPO is expected to be one of the largest in Indian capital market history. Ambani also announced RIL's vision to build powerful, trusted, and affordable Artificial Intelligence, with a dedicated India-focused AI team.
exam_angle: The Jio IPO filing is relevant for understanding capital market regulations (SEBI's IPO framework, DRHP process) and the significance of large domestic listings for deepening Indian equity markets — key for GS Paper 3 Economy.
key_facts: Company: Jio Platforms (subsidiary of Reliance Industries Ltd)
DRHP filing date with SEBI: June 19, 2026
Announced at: RIL AGM 2026 by Mukesh Ambani
Regulator: Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)

title: El Niño Poses Risks to India's Economic Growth and Inflation Trajectory
summary: Analysts warn that the potential onset of El Niño weather conditions could adversely impact India's agricultural output by disrupting the monsoon, posing a dual threat of slower GDP growth and elevated food inflation. El Niño — a periodic warming of Pacific Ocean surface temperatures — historically correlates with below-normal rainfall in India, affecting kharif crop production and rural incomes. The Reserve Bank of India and government policymakers monitor El Niño forecasts closely while calibrating monetary and fiscal policy. India's dependence on monsoon-fed agriculture makes El Niño a macro-economic risk factor.
exam_angle: El Niño and its impact on Indian monsoon, food inflation, and RBI monetary policy is a classic intersection of Environment and Economy — highly relevant for GS Paper 3 both under Agriculture and Macro-economy sections.
key_facts: Phenomenon: El Niño — warming of equatorial Pacific Ocean surface temperatures
Impact on India: Below-normal monsoon rainfall, reduced kharif output
Economic risks: Lower GDP growth, higher food inflation
Monitoring agencies: IMD (weather), RBI (monetary policy calibration)

title: Indian IT Stocks Fall up to 7% as Accenture Cuts Revenue Outlook
summary: Leading Indian IT companies saw sharp stock declines of up to 7% after global IT services major Accenture reduced its revenue outlook for the fiscal year, citing weaker-than-expected client spending on technology. The development triggered a broader market selloff, with the Sensex falling approximately 700 points and Nifty closing above 24,000. Accenture's earnings are closely watched as a bellwether for global IT spending trends, which directly affect Indian IT exporters like TCS, Infosys, and Wipro. The event highlighted the vulnerability of India's IT sector to global macroeconomic headwinds.
exam_angle: Relevant for understanding India's IT export sector, its global linkages, and how international business cycles affect domestic capital markets — important for GS Paper 3 (Economy) on services sector and balance of payments.
key_facts: Trigger: Accenture cut its annual revenue outlook
Market impact: Sensex fell ~700 points; Indian IT stocks dropped up to 7%
Total market cap erosion cited: ~Rs 1.3 lakh crore
Indian IT companies affected: TCS, Infosys, Wipro among others

title: OpenAI Expands India Team, Prioritises Language and Culture for Voice AI
summary: OpenAI is expanding its India-based team as part of a 'mission critical' initiative, with a specific focus on Indian languages and cultural context to improve its voice AI assistant's performance for Indian users. The expansion reflects the strategic importance of India as a large and linguistically diverse market for AI products. OpenAI aims to develop voice assistant capabilities that are locally relevant across multiple Indian languages. This aligns with India's National Language Translation Mission (NLTM) and the broader push for AI in regional languages.
exam_angle: Relevant to India's AI policy ecosystem, the push for indigenous language AI, and India's Digital India/BharatNet goals. Connects to GS Paper 3 topics on Artificial Intelligence, technology governance, and India's data/language diversity.
key_facts: Company: OpenAI
Initiative: Expanding India team for language and culture-focused voice AI
Focus: Indian languages and cultural localisation of voice assistant
Relevance: India's multilingual diversity and AI governance policy

title: Nita Ambani Reiterates Vision to Host Olympics in India at RIL AGM 2026
summary: At the Reliance Industries AGM 2026, Nita Ambani, IOC member and Chairperson of the Indian Olympic Association, outlined a comprehensive sports roadmap for India and reiterated her vision of bringing the Olympic Games to India. She emphasized investment in sports infrastructure, athlete development, and India's bid to position itself as a global sports hub. India has been exploring the possibility of hosting the 2036 Summer Olympics, with the government providing policy support. The announcement reinforces India's international sports diplomacy ambitions.
exam_angle: India's bid for the 2036 Olympics is a recurring current affairs topic linking sports diplomacy, infrastructure policy, and India's soft power — relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR and Governance) and GS Paper 3 (Infrastructure).
key_facts: Announced at: RIL AGM 2026
Person: Nita Ambani (IOC Member, Indian Olympic Association Chairperson)
Vision: Hosting Olympic Games in India (target year: 2036 Summer Olympics bid)
Context: India's sports diplomacy and infrastructure development push
